title: Cheesy Mexican Noodle Casserole
description: A creamy, cheesy, and spicy Mexican-inspired noodle casserole perfect for a weeknight dinner. This comforting dish combines the flavors of Mexico with the simplicity of a homemade casserole.
instructions: Cook the noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the cumin, chili powder, and salsa. Cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes to the skillet and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked noodles, ground beef mixture, and 1 cup of the shredded cheddar cheese. Mix well.
Transfer the noodle mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Top the noodle mixture with the remaining 1/2 cup of shredded cheddar cheese and the shredded Monterey Jack cheese.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
